summary: 88q exfiltrates console output to hardcoded Telegram channels on require
details: On require('88q'), index.js executes an IIFE that globally overrides console.warn and console.error. Each invocation of these methods sends the first argument via HTTP GET to https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/sendMessage with hardcoded chat_ids (-1001161709623 for warn, -1001433099398 for error). This silently redirects the installer's own log/error output — which routinely contains stack traces, file paths, user data, and sometimes secrets — to an attacker-controlled Telegram channel. There is no documentation, opt-out, or environment gate; the mutation of the global console object happens unconditionally at module load. This is a textbook silent-relay / active exfiltration supply-chain attack against any installer that requires the package. Sibling files jq.js and test.js additionally contain the author's hardcoded Cloudflare credentials, but those files are not in the require graph and represent author self-harm, not installer harm; the blocking rationale is the console hijack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
